items.blurb: Tiny illustrated envelopes from Shoichiro Tobimatsu that turn stamps, clips and pocket-money surprises into little framed treasures.
A palm-sized birthday card starring a swan and its cake, drawn in Tobimatsu's storybook style. Envelope included, charm guaranteed.
Bears running a bakery on a card smaller than your palm — the sweetest possible way to say happy birthday. Matching envelope inside.
A little cat proffers a bouquet on this mini thank-you card — gratitude, but make it storybook-cute. Envelope included.
Tobimatsu's cozy cat says thanks so you don't have to find the words. Mini card with matching envelope, maximum warmth.
Eight sheets, four envelopes, endless excuses to send tiny letters — Tobimatsu's framed illustrations make snail mail feel like art.
Yohaku's moody gray sticker roll: five metres of translucent collage magic for journal spreads that whisper instead of shout.
A hundred flower-strewn square sheets from tiny Tokyo brand Seitosha — your grocery lists deserve a soundtrack of blooms.
William Morris's most famous thrushes, now in wide washi form — wrap gifts and journal pages in Victorian garden drama.
mt soaks paper in coloured resin for these Ganshin tapes — fifteen deep, velvety shades that stay vivid roll after roll.
Die-cut clear tape that drapes like tiny shimmering curtains across planners, jars and gift wrap. HITOTOKI does it again.
Century-old Yoshikawa Paper reworked traditional washi so modern pens glide on it — a ruled memo pad with serious heritage.
Eraser-stamp artist eric inked this anniversary tote and keychain duo in navy silkscreen — a wearable love letter to small joys.
Postcards you colour in yourself, drawn by stamp artist eric for a tenth-birthday celebration — mail that starts as a blank page.
eric took her eraser stamps to New York and came back with these — little skyline-and-daily-delight stickers for your journal.
A paper clip that spins 360 degrees so eric's tiny illustration always faces the right way up. Desk jewellery, essentially.
eric's daruma paper clip rotates to clip from any angle — a lucky little wish-keeper for your paperwork.
Seventy-two letterpress cards in twelve designs, pressed in Osaka and boxed like tiny artworks. The paper nerd's treasure chest.
Tocorocomugi's gentle cats deliver birthday wishes on a card the size of a biscuit. Matching envelope, oversized charm.
Cats, flowers and music on one postcard — Tocorocomugi's warm little dance party you can drop in the mail.
A duo of mt tapes in blue and pastel gray, milled from recycled coffee filters — soft colours with a quietly virtuous backstory.
A duo of mt tapes in pastel lemon and fresh green, milled from recycled coffee filters — soft colours with a quietly virtuous backstory.
A duo of mt tapes in rose pink and uguisu green, milled from recycled coffee filters — soft colours with a quietly virtuous backstory.
A duo of mt tapes in yellow and lavender, milled from recycled coffee filters — soft colours with a quietly virtuous backstory.
A duo of mt tapes in grayish purple and water blue, milled from recycled coffee filters — soft colours with a quietly virtuous backstory.
Curling scrolls and blooms from William Morris, shrunk onto mt washi tape — Victorian wallpaper elegance for planners, parcels and everything between.

Strawberry Thief's birds, up close from William Morris, shrunk onto mt washi tape — Victorian wallpaper elegance for planners, parcels and everything between.
Uni-ball One's summer-only barrels in 0.5mm — that famously dense gel ink dressed for the season. Blink and they're gone.
Uni-ball One's summer-only barrels in 0.38mm — that famously dense gel ink dressed for the season. Blink and they're gone.
Yoshikawa Paper's Sutenai memo pad turns would-be waste into ruled washi sheets — guilt-free scribbling since 1909, basically.
An A5 ruled note pad of fully recycled washi from Yoshikawa Paper — heritage craft with a zero-waste conscience.
Sutenai means don't throw away — this plain A5 notebook is spun entirely from recycled washi by a century-old Japanese mill.
A square hardcover notebook in 100% recycled washi — Yoshikawa Paper proving sustainability can feel this luxurious.
Torinoko washi reengineered for ballpoints and gels — an A5 ruled pad where tradition finally meets your everyday pen.
A plain A5 notebook of Torinoko washi, crafted so modern ink behaves on paper made the old way.
Neue Gray's softly tinted pages are easy on the eyes and kind to your ink colours — an A5 pad artists asked for by name.
The pocket A6 version of Neue Gray: gentle gray paper that flatters every ink and never glares back at you.
Neue Gray in plain A5 — a calm gray-paged notebook designed to keep colours vivid and eye strain nonexistent.
Artist Moriyue's Beach Combing washi tape brings painterly little scenes to your journal margins — subtle, dreamy, very collectable.
Artist Moriyue's Blue Layer washi tape brings painterly little scenes to your journal margins — subtle, dreamy, very collectable.
Artist Moriyue's Fluorite washi tape brings painterly little scenes to your journal margins — subtle, dreamy, very collectable.
Fifty sheets across five of eric's tiny town scenes — to-do lists suddenly feel like postcards from a friendlier universe.
eric's stamped fruit motifs across fifty memo sheets in five designs — the juiciest way to leave someone a note.
A heart-shaped embroidered turtle from The Superior Labor — stitch-on personality for notebook covers, jackets and pouches.
A heart-shaped embroidered crane from The Superior Labor — stitch-on personality for notebook covers, jackets and pouches.
Fifty-four flake stickers with gold foil accents that catch the light — eric's stamp art at its most glimmery.
Twelve fresh Versafine Clair shades: oil-based, fast-drying, waterproof once set — crisp stamping without the smudge anxiety.
Two A5 sheets of mizutama's cake-shop stickers, including one giant design made for happy scissor sessions. Sweet in every sense.
Round sticky notes printed with four full moons from different seasons and skies — lunar phases for your to-do list.
Irofuru loose sheets in A5 grid: paper engineered to make fountain pen inks sheen, shade and generally show off.
Irofuru loose sheets in A5 dot: paper engineered to make fountain pen inks sheen, shade and generally show off.
Irofuru loose sheets in A5 plain: paper engineered to make fountain pen inks sheen, shade and generally show off.
An A5 plain Irofuru notebook whose soft-touch paper coaxes maximum sheen and shading out of every fountain pen ink.
An A5 dot Irofuru notebook whose soft-touch paper coaxes maximum sheen and shading out of every fountain pen ink.
An A5 grid Irofuru notebook whose soft-touch paper coaxes maximum sheen and shading out of every fountain pen ink.
Tomoe River's featherweight 52g paper in an A5 dot notebook — impossibly thin, famously fountain-pen friendly.
Tomoe River's featherweight 52g paper in an A5 plain notebook — impossibly thin, famously fountain-pen friendly.
A washable all-cotton coaster scattered with red blooms — the cozy landing pad your tea mug has been waiting for.
Fifteen translucent sheets dotted with candy-coloured bubbles — Seitosha's see-through memos layer beautifully in journals.
A hundred square sheets of confetti-bright bubbles from Tokyo's tiny Seitosha studio — small notes with big personality.
A hundred square sheets of cheerful red flowers from Tokyo's tiny Seitosha studio — small notes with big personality.
A hundred square sheets of dreamy blue flowers from Tokyo's tiny Seitosha studio — small notes with big personality.
Eighty round-cornered sticky notes in two moonlit shades — the Sunny Spot series makes even reminders feel gentle.
